Code Summary: This is a GPU-accelerated Graph Neural Network pipeline designed to analyze over 1.2 million single cells from brain tissue to study Alzheimer's Disease. The code implements custom PyTorch-based preprocessing (highly variable gene selection, PCA) to overcome memory limitations, constructs biological graphs (cell-cell and gene-gene networks), and trains GNN models (GCN, GAT, Graph Auto-Encoders) to identify disease-related cellular transitions and gene regulatory networks.
Data Types: - Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) data - Brain transcriptomic data from human cortex - Clinical/disease state annotations (Alzheimer's Disease vs healthy) - Gene expression matrices - Cell metadata
Tooling: - PyTorch (deep learning framework and custom GPU kernels) - PyTorch Geometric (PyG) (GNN layers and graph data structures) - PyTorch Lightning (training loop abstraction and distributed training) - Scanpy (single-cell analysis toolkit) - AnnData (annotated data matrices with HDF5 backing) - H5py (HDF5 file operations) - CUDA (GPU acceleration) - NumPy and Pandas (data manipulation) - FastAPI (model serving) - Docker (containerization) - Kubernetes (deployment) - Conda (environment management)
